1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.logger.api.initKtLintKLogger
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule
  5→import mu.KotlinLogging
  6→import org.ec4j.core.model.Property
  7→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType
  8→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType.PropertyValue
  9→
 10→private val LOGGER = KotlinLogging.logger {}.initKtLintKLogger()
 11→
 12→/**
 13→ * Loaded [Property]s from `.editorconfig` files.
 14→ */
 15→public data class EditorConfig(
 16→    private val properties: Map<String, Property> = emptyMap(),
 17→) {
 18→    public constructor(vararg properties: Property) : this(
 19→        properties.associateBy { property -> property.name },
 20→    )
 21→
 22→    private val codeStyle: CodeStyleValue
 23→        get() =
 24→            with(CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY) {
 25→                // Prevent printing a lot of warnings when the code_style_value is not defined explicitly by not using the function to get
 26→                // the default value for the property
 27→                type
 28→                    .getPropertyValue(type.name)
 29→                    .parsed
 30→                    ?: defaultValue
 31→            }
 32→
 33→    /**
 34→     * Gets the value of [editorConfigProperty] from [EditorConfig] provided that the name of the property is identical to the name of the
 35→     * type of the property. If the value is not found, the default value for the matching code style is returned.
 36→     */
 37→    public operator fun <T> get(editorConfigProperty: EditorConfigProperty<T>): T {
 38→        when {
 39→            editorConfigProperty.deprecationError != null ->
 40→                throw DeprecatedEditorConfigPropertyException(
 41→                    "Property '${editorConfigProperty.name}' is disallowed: ${editorConfigProperty.deprecationError}",
 42→                )
 43→
 44→            editorConfigProperty.deprecationWarning != null ->
 45→                LOGGER.warn { "Property '${editorConfigProperty.name}' is deprecated: ${editorConfigProperty.deprecationWarning}" }
 46→        }
 47→        val property = properties.getOrElse(editorConfigProperty.name) {
 48→            throw IllegalStateException(
 49→                "Property '${editorConfigProperty.name}' can not be retrieved from this EditorConfig. Note that the EditorConfig which " +
 50→                    "is provided to class '${Rule::class.qualifiedName}' only contains the properties which are defined in the property " +
 51→                    "'${Rule::usesEditorConfigProperties.name}'.",
 52→            )
 53→        }
 54→
 55→        editorConfigProperty
 56→            .propertyMapper
 57→            ?.invoke(property, codeStyle)
 58→            ?.let { newValue ->
 59→                // If the property value is remapped to a non-null value then return it immediately.
 60→                val originalValue = property.sourceValue
 61→                if (newValue.toString() != originalValue) {
 62→                    LOGGER.trace {
 63→                        "Value of '.editorconfig' property '${editorConfigProperty.name}' is remapped " +
 64→                            "from '$originalValue' to '$newValue'"
 65→                    }
 66→                }
 67→                return newValue
 68→            }
 69→
 70→        return if (property.isUnset) {
 71→            editorConfigProperty
 72→                .getDefaultValue()
 73→                .also {
 74→                    LOGGER.trace {
 75→                        "No value of '.editorconfig' property '${editorConfigProperty.name}' was found. Value has been defaulted to " +
 76→                            "'$it'. Setting the value explicitly in '.editorconfig' removes this message from the log."
 77→                    }
 78→                }
 79→        } else {
 80→            // In case the EditorConfig is loaded incorrectly it can happen that the type field of the loaded property is null. Getting the
 81→            // value via "property.getValueAs()" can lead to a class cast exception as the actual type of the value returned is transformed
 82→            // to a String instead of type T. Getting the value via the editorConfigProperty type is safe as it can not be null.
 83→            editorConfigProperty.getParsedValueOrDefault() ?: editorConfigProperty.getDefaultValue()
 84→        }
 85→    }
 86→
 87→    /**
 88→     * Gets the value of the property with [propertyType] and name [propertyName] from [EditorConfig]. Returns null if the type is not
 89→     * found. Also, returns null when no property with the name is found.
 90→     */
 91→    public fun <T> getEditorConfigValueOrNull(
 92→        propertyType: PropertyType<T>,
 93→        propertyName: String,
 94→    ): T? = propertyType.getPropertyValue(propertyName).parsed
 95→
 96→    private fun <T> EditorConfigProperty<T>.getDefaultValue() =
 97→        when (codeStyle) {
 98→            CodeStyleValue.android -> androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue
 99→            CodeStyleValue.android_studio -> androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue
100→            CodeStyleValue.official -> intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue
101→            CodeStyleValue.intellij_idea -> intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue
102→            CodeStyleValue.ktlint_official -> ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue
103→        }
104→
105→    /**
106→     * Checks whether a property with name [propertyName] is defined.
107→     */
108→    public fun contains(propertyName: String): Boolean = properties.contains(propertyName)
109→
110→    /**
111→     * Gets the parsed value of the property with type [PropertyType]. Returns null if the property type is not found. Can only be used for
112→     * properties for which the name of the property is identical to the name of the type.
113→     */
114→    private fun <T> EditorConfigProperty<T>.getParsedValueOrDefault(): T? =
115→        this.type.getParsedValueOrDefault(this.type.name, this.defaultValue)
116→
117→    /**
118→     * Gets the parsed value of the property with type [PropertyType] and name [propertyName]. Returns null if the property type is not
119→     * found. Intended to be used for properties for which the name is not identical to the name of the property type.
120→     */
121→    private fun <T> PropertyType<T>.getParsedValueOrDefault(
122→        propertyName: String,
123→        defaultValue: T,
124→    ): T =
125→        getPropertyValue(propertyName)
126→            .let { propertyValue ->
127→                if (propertyValue.isValid) {
128→                    propertyValue.parsed
129→                } else {
130→                    LOGGER.warn {
131→                        "Editorconfig property '$propertyName' contains an invalid value '${propertyValue.source}'. The default value " +
132→                            "'$defaultValue' is used instead."
133→                    }
134→                    defaultValue
135→                }
136→            }
137→
138→    private fun <T> PropertyType<T>.getPropertyValue(propertyName: String): PropertyValue<T> =
139→        parse(
140→            properties[propertyName]?.sourceValue,
141→        )
142→
143→    /**
144→     * Maps all properties with given [mapper] to a collection.
145→     */
146→    public fun <T> map(mapper: (Property) -> T): Collection<T> =
147→        properties
148→            .values
149→            .map { mapper(it) }
150→
151→    /**
152→     * Adds the given [EditorConfigProperty]'s with its default value for the active code style to the [EditorConfig] if the property does
153→     * not yet exist.
154→     */
155→    public fun addPropertiesWithDefaultValueIfMissing(vararg defaultEditorConfigProperties: EditorConfigProperty<*>): EditorConfig {
156→        val editorConfigProperties = defaultEditorConfigProperties.toSet()
157→        requireSingularIdentities(editorConfigProperties)
158→        return editorConfigProperties
159→            .defaultProperties()
160→            .plus(
161→                // Overwrite default properties with values that were actually already defined in the EditorConfig
162→                properties,
163→            ).let { EditorConfig(it) }
164→    }
165→
166→    /**
167→     * Creates an [EditorConfig] containing [editorConfigProperties] only. Retains the value of the property when defined in the original
168→     * [EditorConfig]. If the property does not exist in the original [EditorConfig], it will be added and its value is set to the default
169→     * value of the active code style.
170→     */
171→    public fun filterBy(editorConfigProperties: Set<EditorConfigProperty<*>>): EditorConfig {
172→        requireSingularIdentities(editorConfigProperties)
173→        return editorConfigProperties
174→            .defaultProperties()
175→            .plus(
176→                // Overwrite default properties with values that were actually already defined in the EditorConfig
177→                properties.filterBy(editorConfigProperties.map { it.name }),
178→            ).let { EditorConfig(it) }
179→    }
180→
181→    private fun requireSingularIdentities(editorConfigProperties: Set<EditorConfigProperty<*>>) {
182→        val editorConfigPropertiesWithMultipleIdentities =
183→            editorConfigProperties
184→                .groupBy { it.name }
185→                .filterValues { groupedEditorConfigProperties ->
186→                    groupedEditorConfigProperties
187→                        .distinctBy { it.hashCode() }
188→                        .count() > 1
189→                }
190→        require(editorConfigPropertiesWithMultipleIdentities.isEmpty()) {
191→            editorConfigPropertiesWithMultipleIdentities
192→                .map { (name, editorConfigProperties) ->
193→                    editorConfigProperties
194→                        .joinToString(
195→                            separator = "\n",
196→                            prefix = "Found multiple editorconfig properties with name '$name' but having distinct identities:\n",
197→                        ) { "  - $it" }
198→                }.joinToString(separator = "\n")
199→        }
200→    }
201→
202→    private fun Set<EditorConfigProperty<*>>.defaultProperties(): Map<String, Property> {
203→        return associate { editorConfigProperty ->
204→            editorConfigProperty
205→                .writeDefaultValue()
206→                .let { editorConfigProperty.name to editorConfigProperty.toPropertyWithValue(it) }
207→        }
208→    }
209→
210→    private fun <T> EditorConfigProperty<T>.writeDefaultValue() = propertyWriter(getDefaultValue())
211→
212→    private fun <T> EditorConfigProperty<T>.toPropertyWithValue(value: String): Property =
213→        Property
214→            .builder()
215→            .type(type)
216→            .name(name)
217→            .value(value)
218→            .build()
219→
220→    private fun Map<String, Property>.filterBy(editorConfigProperties: List<String>): Map<String, Property> =
221→        filterKeys { it in editorConfigProperties }
222→}
223→
224→private class DeprecatedEditorConfigPropertyException(message: String) : RuntimeException(message)
225→

content.text:
Now I understand the issue completely. The `EditorConfigProperty` class supports code-style specific default values via `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue`, `intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue`, and `androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue`. 

The fix is to add `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue = emptyList()` to the `IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND` property so that the `ktlint_official` code style doesn't allow any wildcard imports by default (unlike `intellij_idea` and `android_studio` which should keep the default `java.util.*,kotlinx.android.synthetic.**`).

Let me make the fix:

function.name: edit
function.arguments:
{"file_path": "/project/ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/NoWildcardImportsRule.kt", "old_string": "        public val IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND: EditorConfigProperty<List<PatternEntry>> =
            EditorConfigProperty(
                type = PropertyType(
                    "ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand",
                    "Defines allowed wildcard imports",
                    PACKAGES_TO_USE_ON_DEMAND_IMPORT_PROPERTY_PARSER,
                ),
                /**
                 * Default IntelliJ IDEA style: Use wildcard imports for packages in "java.util", "kotlin.android.synthetic" and
                 * it's subpackages.
                 *
                 * https://github.com/JetBrains/kotlin/blob/ffdab473e28d0d872136b910eb2e0f4beea2e19c/idea/formatter/src/org/jetbrains/kotlin/idea/core/formatter/KotlinCodeStyleSettings.java#L81-L82
                 */
                defaultValue = parseAllowedWildcardImports("java.util.*,kotlinx.android.synthetic.**"),
                propertyWriter = { it.joinToString(separator = ",") },
            )", "new_string": "        public val IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND: EditorConfigProperty<List<PatternEntry>> =
            EditorConfigProperty(
                type = PropertyType(
                    "ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand",
                    "Defines allowed wildcard imports",
                    PACKAGES_TO_USE_ON_DEMAND_IMPORT_PROPERTY_PARSER,
                ),
                /**
                 * Default IntelliJ IDEA style: Use wildcard imports for packages in "java.util", "kotlin.android.synthetic" and
                 * it's subpackages.
                 *
                 * https://github.com/JetBrains/kotlin/blob/ffdab473e28d0d872136b910eb2e0f4beea2e19c/idea/formatter/src/org/jetbrains/kotlin/idea/core/formatter/KotlinCodeStyleSettings.java#L81-L82
                 */
                defaultValue = parseAllowedWildcardImports("java.util.*,kotlinx.android.synthetic.**"),
                ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue = emptyList(),
                propertyWriter = { it.joinToString(separator = ",") },
            )"}

  8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules.internal.importordering.PatternEntry

 13→public class NoWildcardImportsRule :

 26→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(

10→/**
11→ * Tests for NoWildcardImportsRule with ktlint_official code style.
12→ *
13→ * In ktlint_official code style, no wildcard imports are allowed by default (not even java.util.* or
14→ * kotlinx.android.synthetic.** which are allowed in intellij_idea and android_studio code styles).
15→ */
16→class NoWildcardImportsRuleKtlintOfficialCodeStyleTest {
17→    private val noWildcardImportsRuleAssertThat = assertThatRule { NoWildcardImportsRule() }
18→

20→    inner class `Given ktlint_official code style` {
21→        @Test
22→        fun `Wildcard import for java_util is detected`() {

41→        }

50→                """.trimIndent()
51→            noWildcardImportsRuleAssertThat(code)
52→                .withEditorConfigOverride(CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY to ktlint_official)
53→                .hasLintViolationsWithoutAutoCorrect(

 13→public class NoWildcardImportsRule :

 24→    }

 26→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 27→        node: ASTNode,
 28→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 29→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 30→    ) {
 31→        if (node.elementType == IMPORT_DIRECTIVE) {
 32→            val importDirective = node.psi as KtImportDirective
 33→            val path = importDirective.importPath ?: return
 34→            if (!path.isAllUnder) return
 35→            if (allowedWildcardImports.none { it.matches(path) }) {
 36→                emit(node.startOffset, "Wildcard import", false)
 37→            }
 38→        }
 39→    }

 41→    public companion object {
 42→        private const val WILDCARD_WITHOUT_SUBPACKAGES = "*"
 43→        private const val WILDCARD_WITH_SUBPACKAGES = "**"
 44→
 45→        private fun parseAllowedWildcardImports(allowedWildcardImports: String): List<PatternEntry> {
 46→            val importsList = allowedWildcardImports.split(",").onEach { it.trim() }
 47→

 60→                    )
 61→                }
 62→            }
 63→        }

 68→                    else -> try {

This rule does not apply in a number of situations. content.text:
The fix is complete. Let me provide a summary of the changes made:

## Summary

The issue requested that the `ktlint_official` code style should NOT allow any wildcard imports by default, unlike the `intellij_idea` and `android_studio` code styles which allow `java.util.*` and `kotlinx.android.synthetic.**` by default.

### Changes Made

**File: `/project/ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/NoWildcardImportsRule.kt`**

Added `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue = emptyList()` to the `IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND` property:

```kotlin
public val IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND: EditorConfigProperty<List<PatternEntry>> =
    EditorConfigProperty(
        type = PropertyType(
            "ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand",
            "Defines allowed wildcard imports",
            PACKAGES_TO_USE_ON_DEMAND_IMPORT_PROPERTY_PARSER,
        ),
        /**
         * Default IntelliJ IDEA style: Use wildcard imports for packages in "java.util", "kotlin.android.synthetic" and
         * it's subpackages.
         *
         * https://github.com/JetBrains/kotlin/blob/ffdab473e28d0d872136b910eb2e0f4beea2e19c/idea/formatter/src/org/jetbrains/kotlin/idea/core/formatter/KotlinCodeStyleSettings.java#L81-L82
         */
        defaultValue = parseAllowedWildcardImports("java.util.*,kotlinx.android.synthetic.**"),
        ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue = emptyList(),  // <-- ADDED THIS LINE
        propertyWriter = { it.joinToString(separator = ",") },
    )
```

**New Test File: `/project/ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/test/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/NoWildcardImportsRuleKtlintOfficialCodeStyleTest.kt`**

Created tests to verify that:
- `java.util.*` is detected as a violation in `ktlint_official` code style
- `kotlinx.android.synthetic.**` is detected as a violation in `ktlint_official` code style
- All wildcard imports are detected in `ktlint_official` code style
- Non-wildcard imports are still allowed

### Behavior After Fix

| Code Style | Default Allowed Wildcard Imports |
|------------|----------------------------------|
| `intellij_idea` | `java.util.*`, `kotlinx.android.synthetic.**` |
| `android_studio` | `java.util.*`, `kotlinx.android.synthetic.**` |
| `ktlint_official` | None (empty list) |

This is a minimal, targeted change that:
1. Resolves the issue for `ktlint_official` code style
2. Maintains backward compatibility for existing `intellij_idea` and `android_studio` code styles
3. All existing tests continue to pass

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig
 2→
 3→import org.ec4j.core.model.Property
 4→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType
 5→
 6→/**
 7→ * Definition of '.editorconfig' property enriched with fields required by the [com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintRuleEngine].
 8→ *
 9→ * Properties can be:
10→ * - universal `.editorconfig` properties defined
11→ *   [here](https://github.com/editorconfig/editorconfig/wiki/EditorConfig-Properties#current-universal-properties)
12→ * - universal IntelliJ IDEA properties defined
13→ *   [here](https://github.com/JetBrains/intellij-community/blob/master/platform/lang-api/src/com/intellij/psi/codeStyle/CommonCodeStyleSettings.java)
14→ * - Kotlin specific properties defined
15→ *   [here](https://github.com/JetBrains/kotlin/blob/master/idea/formatter/src/org/jetbrains/kotlin/idea/core/formatter/KotlinCodeStyleSettings.java)
16→ */
17→public data class EditorConfigProperty<T>(
18→    /**
19→     * Type of property. Could be one of default ones (see [PropertyType.STANDARD_TYPES]) or custom one.
20→     */
21→    public val type: PropertyType<T>,
22→
23→    /**
24→     * Default value for property if it does not exist in loaded properties. This default applies to all code styles
25→     * unless the code style specific default value is set.
26→     */
27→    public val defaultValue: T,
28→
29→    /**
30→     * Default value for property if it does not exist in loaded properties and codestyle 'ktlint_official'. When not
31→     * set, it is defaulted to [defaultValue].
32→     */
33→    public val ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue: T = defaultValue,
34→
35→    /**
36→     * Default value for property if it does not exist in loaded properties and codestyle 'intellij_idea'. When not
37→     * set, it is defaulted to [defaultValue].
38→     */
39→    public val intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue: T = defaultValue,
40→
41→    /**
42→     * Default value for property if it does not exist in loaded properties and codestyle 'android_studio'. When not
43→     * set, it is defaulted to [defaultValue].
44→     */
45→    public val androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue: T = defaultValue,
46→
47→    /**
48→     * If set, it maps the actual value set for the property, to another valid value for that property. See example
49→     * below where
50→     * ```kotlin
51→     * propertyMapper = { property, isAndroidCodeStyle ->
52→     *     when {
53→     *         property == null ->
54→     *             // property is not defined in ".editorconfig" file
55→     *         property.isUnset ->
56→     *             // property is defined in ".editorconfig" file with special value "unset"
57→     *         property.sourceValue == "some-string-value" ->
58→     *             // property is defined in ".editorconfig" file with a value that needs to be remapped to another
59→     *             // valid value. For example the "max_line_length" property accepts value "off" but is remapped to
60→     *             // "-1" in ktlint.
61→     *        else ->
62→     *             property.getValueAs() // or null
63→     *     }
64→     * }
65→     * ```
66→     * In case the lambda returns a null value then, the code style specific default value or the generic [defaultValue]
67→     * is used.
68→     */
69→    public val propertyMapper: ((Property?, CodeStyleValue) -> T?)? = null,
70→
71→    /**
72→     * Custom function that represents [T] as String. Defaults to the standard `toString()` call. Override the
73→     * default implementation in case you need a different behavior than the standard `toString()` (e.g. for
74→     * collections joinToString() is more applicable).
75→     */
76→    public val propertyWriter: (T) -> String = { it.toString() },
77→
78→    /**
79→     * Optional message to be displayed whenever the value of the property is being retrieved while it has been deprecated.
80→     */
81→    val deprecationWarning: String? = null,
82→
83→    /**
84→     * Optional message to be displayed whenever the value of the property is being retrieved while it has been deprecated.
85→     */
86→    val deprecationError: String? = null,
87→
88→    /**
89→     * Name of the property. A property must be named in case multiple properties are defined for the same type.
90→     * Defaults to the name of the type when not set.
91→     */
92→    public val name: String = type.name,
93→)

 118→private val LOGGER = KotlinLogging.logger {}.initKtLintKLogger()
 119→
 120→public class IndentationRule :
 121→    StandardRule(
 122→        id = "indent",
 123→        visitorModifiers = setOf(
 124→            VisitorModifier.RunAsLateAsPossible,
 125→            VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
 126→                ruleId = FUNCTION_SIGNATURE_RULE_ID,
 127→                mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 128→            ),
 129→            VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
 130→                ruleId = TRAILING_COMMA_ON_CALL_SITE_RULE_ID,
 131→                mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 132→            ),
 133→            VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
 134→                ruleId = TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_RULE_ID,
 135→                mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 136→            ),
 137→        ),
 138→        usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(
 139→            CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 140→            INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 141→            INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 142→        ),
 143→    ) {
 144→    private var codeStyle = CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 145→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 146→
 147→    private var line = 1
 148→
 149→    private val indentContextStack: Deque<IndentContext> = LinkedList()
 150→
 151→    private lateinit var stringTemplateIndenter: StringTemplateIndenter
 152→
 153→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 154→        codeStyle = editorConfig[CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY]
 155→        indentConfig = IndentConfig(
 156→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 157→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 158→        )
 159→        if (indentConfig.disabled) {
 160→            stopTraversalOfAST()
 161→        }
 162→    }
 163→
 164→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 165→        node: ASTNode,
 166→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 167→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 168→    ) {
 169→        if (node.isRoot()) {
 170→            // File should not start with a whitespace
 171→            node
 172→                .nextLeaf()
 173→                ?.takeIf { it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() }
 174→                ?.let { whitespaceWithoutNewline ->
 175→                    emit(node.startOffset, "Unexpected indentation", true)
 176→                    if (autoCorrect) {
 177→                        whitespaceWithoutNewline.treeParent.removeChild(whitespaceWithoutNewline)
 178→                    }
 179→                }
 180→            indentContextStack.addLast(startNoIndentZone(node))
 181→        }
 182→
 183→        when {
 184→            node.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() -> {
 185→                line++
 186→                if (indentContextStack.peekLast()?.activated == false) {
 187→                    val lastIndentContext = indentContextStack.removeLast()
 188→                    indentContextStack.addLast(
 189→                        lastIndentContext.copy(activated = true),
 190→                    )
 191→                }
 192→                visitNewLineIndentation(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 193→            }
 194→
 195→            node.elementType == CLASS_BODY ||
 196→                node.elementType == CONTEXT_RECEIVER_LIST ||
 197→                node.elementType == LONG_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY ||
 198→                node.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_CALL_ENTRY ||
 199→                node.elementType == STRING_TEMPLATE ||
 200→                node.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST ->
 201→                startIndentContext(
 202→                    fromAstNode = node,
 203→                    lastChildIndent = "",
 204→                )
 205→
 206→            node.elementType == SECONDARY_CONSTRUCTOR ->
 207→                visitSecondaryConstructor(node)
 208→
 209→            node.elementType == PARENTHESIZED &&
 210→                node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != IF ->
 211→                startIndentContext(node)
 212→
 213→            node.elementType == BINARY_WITH_TYPE ||
 214→                node.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_ENTRY ||
 215→                node.elementType == TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST ||
 216→                node.elementType == TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST ->
 217→                startIndentContext(node)
 218→
 219→            node.elementType == DELEGATED_SUPER_TYPE_ENTRY ||
 220→                node.elementType == ANNOTATED_EXPRESSION ||
 221→                node.elementType == TYPE_REFERENCE ->
 222→                startIndentContext(
 223→                    fromAstNode = node,
 224→                    childIndent = "",
 225→                )
 226→
 227→            node.elementType == IF ->
 228→                visitIf(node)
 229→
 230→            node.elementType == LBRACE ->
 231→                visitLbrace(node)
 232→
 233→            node.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST &&
 234→                node.treeParent.elementType != FUNCTION_LITERAL ->
 235→                startIndentContext(
 236→                    fromAstNode = node,
 237→                    lastChildIndent = "",
 238→                )
 239→
 240→            node.elementType == LPAR &&
 241→                node.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == CONDITION ->
 242→                visitLparBeforeCondition(node)
 243→
 244→            node.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER ->
 245→                visitValueParameter(node)
 246→
 247→            node.elementType == FUN ->
 248→                visitFun(node)
 249→
 250→            node.elementType == CLASS ->
 251→                visitClass(node)
 252→
 253→            node.elementType == OBJECT_DECLARATION ->
 254→                visitObjectDeclaration(node)
 255→
 256→            node.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION ->
 257→                visitBinaryExpression(node)
 258→
 259→            node.elementType == DOT_QUALIFIED_EXPRESSION ||
 260→                node.elementType == SAFE_ACCESS_EXPRESSION ||
 261→                node.elementType == USER_TYPE -> {
 262→                if (codeStyle == ktlint_official &&
 263→                    node.elementType == DOT_QUALIFIED_EXPRESSION &&
 264→                    node.treeParent?.elementType == ARRAY_ACCESS_EXPRESSION &&
 265→                    node.treeParent?.treeParent?.elementType == CALL_EXPRESSION
 266→                ) {
 267→                    // Issue 1540: Deviate and fix from incorrect formatting in IntelliJ IDEA formatting and produce following:
 268→                    // val fooBar2 = foo
 269→                    //    .bar[0] {
 270→                    //        "foobar"
 271→                    //    }
 272→                    startIndentContext(
 273→                        fromAstNode = node.treeParent,
 274→                        toAstNode = node.treeParent.treeParent.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 275→                    )
 276→                } else if (node.treeParent?.elementType != node.elementType) {
 277→                    startIndentContext(node)
 278→                }
 279→            }
 280→
 281→            node.elementType == IDENTIFIER &&
 282→                node.treeParent.elementType == PROPERTY ->
 283→                visitIdentifierInProperty(node)
 284→
 285→            node.elementType == LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY &&
 286→                node.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == CLOSING_QUOTE ->
 287→                visitWhiteSpaceBeforeClosingQuote(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 288→
 289→            node.elementType == WHEN ->
 290→                visitWhen(node)
 291→
 292→            node.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY ->
 293→                visitWhenEntry(node)
 294→
 295→            node.elementType == WHERE_KEYWORD &&
 296→                node.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == TYPE_CONSTRAINT_LIST ->
 297→                visitWhereKeywordBeforeTypeConstraintList(node)
 298→
 299→            node.elementType == KDOC ->
 300→                visitKdoc(node)
 301→
 302→            node.elementType == PROPERTY_ACCESSOR ||
 303→                node.elementType == TYPEALIAS ->
 304→                visitPropertyAccessor(node)
 305→
 306→            node.elementType == FOR ||
 307→                node.elementType == WHILE ->
 308→                visitConditionalLoop(node)
 309→
 310→            node.elementType == LBRACKET ->
 311→                visitLBracket(node)
 312→
 313→            node.elementType == NULLABLE_TYPE ->
 314→                visitNullableType(node)
 315→
 316→            node.elementType == DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION ->
 317→                visitDestructuringDeclaration(node)
 318→
 319→            node.elementType == TRY ->
 320→                visitTryCatchFinally(node)
 321→
 322→            else -> {
 323→                LOGGER.trace { "No processing for ${node.elementType}: ${node.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()}" }
 324→            }
 325→        }
 326→    }
 327→
 328→    private fun visitSecondaryConstructor(node: ASTNode) {
 329→        node
 330→            .findChildByType(CONSTRUCTOR_DELEGATION_CALL)
 331→            ?.let { constructorDelegationCall ->
 332→                val fromAstNode = node.skipLeadingWhitespaceCommentsAndAnnotations()
 333→                val nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 334→                    fromAstNode = constructorDelegationCall,
 335→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 336→
 337→                // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as constructor itself
 338→                if (fromAstNode != node.nextLeaf()) {
 339→                    startIndentContext(
 340→                        fromAstNode = node,
 341→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 342→                        childIndent = "",
 343→                    )
 344→                }
 345→            }
 346→    }
 347→
 348→    private fun visitIf(node: ASTNode) {
 349→        var nextToAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 350→        node
 351→            .findChildByType(ELSE)
 352→            ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 353→                nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 354→                    fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 355→                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 356→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 357→            }
 358→
 359→        node
 360→            .findChildByType(THEN)
 361→            ?.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 362→            ?.nextLeaf()
 363→            ?.let { nodeAfterThenBlock ->
 364→                nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 365→                    fromAstNode = nodeAfterThenBlock,
 366→                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 367→                    childIndent = "",
 368→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 369→            }
 370→        node
 371→            .findChildByType(RPAR)
 372→            ?.nextCodeLeaf()
 373→            ?.let { nodeAfterConditionBlock ->
 374→                nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 375→                    fromAstNode = nodeAfterConditionBlock,
 376→                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 377→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 378→            }
 379→        startIndentContext(
 380→            fromAstNode = node,
 381→            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 382→            lastChildIndent = "", // No indent for the RPAR
 383→        )
 384→    }
 385→
 386→    private fun visitLbrace(node: ASTNode) {
 387→        // Outer indent context
 388→        val rbrace = requireNotNull(
 389→            node.nextSibling { it.elementType == RBRACE },
 390→        ) { "Can not find matching rbrace" }
 391→        startIndentContext(
 392→            fromAstNode = node,
 393→            toAstNode = rbrace,
 394→            firstChildIndent = "",
 395→            lastChildIndent = "",
 396→        )
 397→
 398→        // Inner indent context in reversed order
 399→        node
 400→            .treeParent
 401→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == FUNCTION_LITERAL }
 402→            ?.findChildByType(ARROW)
 403→            ?.let { arrow ->
 404→                startIndentContext(
 405→                    fromAstNode = arrow,
 406→                    toAstNode = rbrace,
 407→                    lastChildIndent = "",
 408→                )
 409→                startIndentContext(
 410→                    fromAstNode = node,
 411→                    toAstNode = arrow.prevCodeLeaf()!!,
 412→                    childIndent = arrow.calculateIndentOfFunctionLiteralParameters(),
 413→                )
 414→            }
 415→    }
 416→
 417→    private fun ASTNode.calculateIndentOfFunctionLiteralParameters() =
 418→        if (codeStyle == ktlint_official || isFirstParameterOfFunctionLiteralPrecededByNewLine()) {
 419→            // val fieldExample =
 420→            //      LongNameClass {
 421→            //              paramA,
 422→            //              paramB,
 423→            //              paramC ->
 424→            //          ClassB(paramA, paramB, paramC)
 425→            //      }
 426→            indentConfig.indent.repeat(2)
 427→        } else {
 428→            // Allow default IntelliJ IDEA formatting:
 429→            // val fieldExample =
 430→            //     LongNameClass { paramA,
 431→            //                     paramB,
 432→            //                     paramC ->
 433→            //         ClassB(paramA, paramB, paramC)
 434→            //     }
 435→            parent(CALL_EXPRESSION)
 436→                ?.let { callExpression ->
 437→                    val textBeforeFirstParameter =
 438→                        callExpression.findChildByType(REFERENCE_EXPRESSION)?.text +
 439→                            " { "
 440→                    " ".repeat(textBeforeFirstParameter.length)
 441→                }
 442→                ?: indentConfig.indent.repeat(2)
 443→        }
 444→
 445→    private fun ASTNode.isFirstParameterOfFunctionLiteralPrecededByNewLine() =
 446→        parent(FUNCTION_LITERAL)
 447→            ?.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST)
 448→            ?.prevSibling { it.textContains('\n') } != null
 449→
 450→    private fun visitLparBeforeCondition(node: ASTNode) {
 451→        startIndentContext(
 452→            fromAstNode = requireNotNull(node.nextLeaf()), // Allow to pickup whitespace before condition
 453→            toAstNode = requireNotNull(node.nextCodeSibling()).lastChildLeafOrSelf(), // Ignore whitespace after condition but before rpar
 454→            nodeIndent = currentIndent() + indentConfig.indent,
 455→            childIndent = "",
 456→        )
 457→    }
 458→
 459→    private fun visitValueParameter(node: ASTNode) {
 460→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 461→        var nextToAstNode: ASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 462→        node
 463→            .findChildByType(EQ)
 464→            ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 465→                nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 466→                    fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 467→                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 468→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 469→            }
 470→
 471→        if (codeStyle == ktlint_official) {
 472→            // Deviate from standard IntelliJ IDEA formatting to allow formatting below:
 473→            //     fun process(
 474→            //         aVariableWithAVeryLongName:
 475→            //             TypeWithAVeryLongNameThatDoesNotFitOnSameLineAsTheVariableName
 476→            //     ): List<Output>
 477→            node
 478→                .findChildByType(COLON)
 479→                ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 480→                    nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 481→                        fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 482→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 483→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 484→                }
 485→        }
 486→
 487→        // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as constructor itself
 488→        val fromAstNode = node.skipLeadingWhitespaceCommentsAndAnnotations()
 489→        if (fromAstNode != node.firstChildNode &&
 490→            node.prevSibling { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() } == null &&
 491→            node == node.treeParent.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER)
 492→        ) {
 493→            nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 494→                fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 495→                toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 496→            ).fromASTNode.prevLeaf { !it.isWhiteSpace() }!!
 497→        } else {
 498→            startIndentContext(
 499→                fromAstNode = node,
 500→                toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 501→                childIndent = "",
 502→            )
 503→        }
 504→    }
 505→
 506→    private fun visitFun(node: ASTNode) {
 507→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 508→        var nextToAstNode: ASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 509→        val eqOrBlock =
 510→            node.findChildByType(EQ)
 511→                ?: node.findChildByType(BLOCK)
 512→        eqOrBlock?.let {
 513→            nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 514→                fromAstNode = eqOrBlock,
 515→                toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 516→            ).prevCodeLeaf()
 517→        }
 518→
 519→        node
 520→            .findChildByType(TYPE_REFERENCE)
 521→            ?.let { typeReference ->
 522→                nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 523→                    fromAstNode = typeReference.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 524→                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 525→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 526→            }
 527→
 528→        // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as function itself
 529→        startIndentContext(
 530→            fromAstNode = node,
 531→            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 532→            childIndent = "",
 533→        )
 534→    }

 536→    private fun visitClass(node: ASTNode) {
 537→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 538→        var nextToAstNode: ASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 539→        node
 540→            .findChildByType(WHERE_KEYWORD)
 541→            ?.let { where ->
 542→                val typeConstraintList =
 543→                    requireNotNull(
 544→                        where.nextCodeSibling(),
 545→                    ) { "Can not find code sibling after WHERE in CLASS" }
 546→                require(
 547→                    typeConstraintList.elementType == TYPE_CONSTRAINT_LIST,
 548→                ) { "Code sibling after WHERE in CLASS is not a TYPE_CONSTRAINT_LIST" }
 549→                nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 550→                    fromAstNode = where.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 551→                    toAstNode = typeConstraintList.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 552→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 553→            }
 554→        node
 555→            .findChildByType(SUPER_TYPE_LIST)
 556→            ?.let { superTypeList ->
 557→                nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 558→                    fromAstNode = superTypeList.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 559→                    toAstNode = superTypeList.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 560→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 561→            }
 562→
 563→        // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as class itself
 564→        startIndentContext(
 565→            fromAstNode = node,
 566→            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 567→            childIndent = "",
 568→        )
 569→    }

 591→    private fun visitBinaryExpression(node: ASTNode) {
 592→        if (isPartOfBinaryExpressionWrappedInCondition(node)) {
 593→            // Complex binary expression are nested in such a way that the indent context of the condition
 594→            // wrapper is not the last node on the stack
 595→            val conditionIndentContext =
 596→                indentContextStack.last { it.fromASTNode.elementType != BINARY_EXPRESSION }
 597→            // Create new indent context for the remainder (operator and right-hand side) of the binary expression
 598→            startIndentContext(
 599→                fromAstNode = node.findChildByType(OPERATION_REFERENCE)!!,
 600→                toAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 601→                nodeIndent = conditionIndentContext.nodeIndent,
 602→                childIndent = conditionIndentContext.childIndent,
 603→            )
 604→            startIndentContext(node.firstChildNode)
 605→        } else if (node.treeParent?.elementType != BINARY_EXPRESSION ||
 606→            node.findChildByType(OPERATION_REFERENCE)?.firstChildNode?.elementType == ELVIS
 607→        ) {
 608→            startIndentContext(node)
 609→        }
 610→    }

 617→    }

 806→    private fun ASTNode.skipLeadingWhitespaceCommentsAndAnnotations(): ASTNode {
 807→        require(elementType == SECONDARY_CONSTRUCTOR || elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER)
 808→        return findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST)
 809→            ?.let { modifierList ->
 810→                modifierList
 811→                    .children()
 812→                    .firstOrNull { !it.isWhiteSpace() && !it.isPartOfComment() && it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
 813→                    ?: modifierList.nextCodeSibling()
 814→            }
 815→            ?: children().firstOrNull { !it.isWhiteSpace() && !it.isPartOfComment() }
 816→            ?: this
 817→    }
 818→
 819→    private fun ASTNode.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(): ASTNode {
 820→        var fromAstNode: ASTNode? = this
 821→        while (fromAstNode?.prevLeaf() != null &&
 822→            (fromAstNode.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpace() || fromAstNode.prevLeaf()?.isPartOfComment() == true)
 823→        ) {
 824→            fromAstNode = fromAstNode.prevLeaf()
 825→        }
 826→        return fromAstNode!!
 827→    }

 829→    private fun isPartOfBinaryExpressionWrappedInCondition(node: ASTNode) =
 830→        node
 831→            .parents()
 832→            .takeWhile { it.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION || it.elementType == CONDITION }
 833→            .lastOrNull()
 834→            ?.elementType == CONDITION

 836→    private fun currentIndent() =
 837→        indentContextStack
 838→            .peekLast()
 839→            .indent()

 841→    private fun startIndentContext(
 842→        fromAstNode: ASTNode,
 843→        toAstNode: ASTNode = fromAstNode.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 844→        nodeIndent: String = currentIndent(),
 845→        childIndent: String = indentConfig.indent,
 846→        firstChildIndent: String = childIndent,
 847→        lastChildIndent: String = childIndent,
 848→    ): IndentContext =
 849→        IndentContext(
 850→            fromASTNode = fromAstNode,
 851→            toASTNode = toAstNode,
 852→            nodeIndent = nodeIndent,
 853→            firstChildIndent = firstChildIndent,
 854→            childIndent = childIndent,
 855→            lastChildIndent = lastChildIndent,
 856→        ).also { newIndentContext ->
 857→            LOGGER.trace {
 858→                val nodeIndentLevel = indentConfig.indentLevelFrom(newIndentContext.nodeIndent)
 859→                val childIndentLevel = indentConfig.indentLevelFrom(newIndentContext.childIndent)
 860→                "Create new indent context (same as parent) with level ($nodeIndentLevel, $childIndentLevel) for " +
 861→                    "${fromAstNode.elementType}: ${newIndentContext.nodes}"
 862→            }
 863→            indentContextStack.addLast(newIndentContext)
 864→        }
 865→
 866→    override fun afterVisitChildNodes(
 867→        node: ASTNode,
 868→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 869→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 870→    ) {
 871→        while (indentContextStack.peekLast()?.toASTNode == node) {
 872→            LOGGER.trace {
 873→                val indentContext = indentContextStack.peekLast()
 874→                val nodeIndentLevel = indentConfig.indentLevelFrom(indentContext.nodeIndent)
 875→                val childIndentLevel = indentConfig.indentLevelFrom(indentContext.childIndent)
 876→                "Remove indent context with level ($nodeIndentLevel, $childIndentLevel) for ${indentContext.fromASTNode.elementType}: " +
 877→                    "${indentContext.nodes}"
 878→            }
 879→            indentContextStack
 880→                .removeLast()
 881→                .also {
 882→                    LOGGER.trace {
 883→                        val indentContext = indentContextStack.peekLast()
 884→                        val nodeIndentLevel = indentConfig.indentLevelFrom(indentContext.nodeIndent)
 885→                        val childIndentLevel = indentConfig.indentLevelFrom(indentContext.childIndent)
 886→                        "Last indent context with level ($nodeIndentLevel, $childIndentLevel) for " +
 887→                            "${indentContext.fromASTNode.elementType}: ${indentContext.nodes}"
 888→                    }
 889→                }
 890→        }
 891→    }
 892→
 893→    override fun afterLastNode() {
 894→        require(indentContextStack.isEmpty()) {
 895→            indentContextStack
 896→                .joinToString(prefix = "Stack should be empty:\n\t", separator = "\n\t") { it.toString() }
 897→        }
 898→    }
 899→
 900→    private fun visitNewLineIndentation(
 901→        node: ASTNode,
 902→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 903→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 904→    ) {
 905→        if (node.ignoreIndent()) {
 906→            return
 907→        }
 908→
 909→        val normalizedNodeIndent = node.normalizedIndent(emit)
 910→        val expectedIndentation = node.expectedIndent()
 911→        val text = node.text
 912→        val nodeIndent = text.substringAfterLast("\n")
 913→        if (nodeIndent != normalizedNodeIndent || normalizedNodeIndent != expectedIndentation) {
 914→            if (normalizedNodeIndent != expectedIndentation) {
 915→                emit(
 916→                    node.startOffset + text.length - nodeIndent.length,
 917→                    "Unexpected indentation (${normalizedNodeIndent.length}) (should be ${expectedIndentation.length})",
 918→                    true,
 919→                )
 920→            } else {
 921→                // Indentation was at correct level but contained invalid indent characters. This violation has already
 922→                // been emitted.
 923→            }
 924→            LOGGER.trace {
 925→                "Line $line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "changed indentation to ${expectedIndentation.length} " +
 926→                    "(from ${normalizedNodeIndent.length}) for ${node.elementType}: ${node.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()}"
 927→            }
 928→            if (autoCorrect) {
 929→                (node as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(
 930→                    text.substringBeforeLast("\n") + "\n" + expectedIndentation,
 931→                )
 932→            }
 933→        } else {
 934→            node.processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded()
 935→        }
 936→    }
 937→
 938→    private fun ASTNode.ignoreIndent(): Boolean {
 939→        val nextLeaf = nextLeaf()
 940→        if (text.endsWith("\n") && nextLeaf.isStartOfRawStringLiteral()) {
 941→            processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded()
 942→            return true // raw strings (""") are allowed at column 0
 943→        }
 944→
 945→        nextLeaf
 946→            ?.parent({ it.psi is PsiComment }, strict = false)
 947→            ?.let { comment ->
 948→                if (text.endsWith("\n")) {
 949→                    processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded()
 950→                    return true // comments are allowed at column 0
 951→                }
 952→                if (comment.textContains('\n') && comment.elementType == BLOCK_COMMENT) {
 953→                    // FIXME: while we cannot assume any kind of layout inside a block comment,
 954→                    // `/*` and `*/` can still be indented
 955→                    processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded()
 956→                    return true
 957→                }
 958→            }
 959→        return false
 960→    }
 961→
 962→    private fun ASTNode?.isStartOfRawStringLiteral(): Boolean = this != null && this.elementType == OPEN_QUOTE && this.text == "\"\"\""
 963→
 964→    private fun ASTNode.processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded() =
 965→        LOGGER.trace { "No indentation change required for $elementType: ${textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()}" }
 966→
 967→    private fun ASTNode.expectedIndent(): String {
 968→        val lastIndexContext = indentContextStack.peekLast()
 969→        val nextLeaf = nextLeaf()
 970→        val adjustedChildIndent =
 971→            when {
 972→                this == lastIndexContext.fromASTNode.firstChildLeafOrSelf() ||
 973→                    nextLeaf == lastIndexContext.fromASTNode.firstChildLeafOrSelf() ->
 974→                    lastIndexContext.firstChildIndent
 975→
 976→                this == lastIndexContext.toASTNode ||
 977→                    nextLeaf == lastIndexContext.toASTNode ->
 978→                    lastIndexContext.lastChildIndent
 979→
 980→                else -> lastIndexContext.childIndent
 981→            }
 982→        return lastIndexContext.nodeIndent + adjustedChildIndent
 983→    }
 984→
 985→    private fun ASTNode.normalizedIndent(emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit): String {
 986→        val nodeIndent = text.substringAfterLast("\n")
 987→        return when (indentConfig.indentStyle) {
 988→            SPACE -> {
 989→                if ('\t' in nodeIndent) {
 990→                    emit(
 991→                        startOffset + text.length - nodeIndent.length,
 992→                        "Unexpected tab character(s)",
 993→                        true,
 994→                    )
 995→                    indentConfig.toNormalizedIndent(nodeIndent)
 996→                } else {
 997→                    nodeIndent
 998→                }
 999→            }
1000→
1001→            TAB -> {
1002→                val acceptableTrailingSpaces = acceptableTrailingSpaces()
1003→                val nodeIndentWithoutAcceptableTrailingSpaces = nodeIndent.removeSuffix(acceptableTrailingSpaces)
1004→                if (' ' in nodeIndentWithoutAcceptableTrailingSpaces) {
1005→                    emit(
1006→                        startOffset + text.length - nodeIndent.length,
1007→                        "Unexpected space character(s)",
1008→                        true,
1009→                    )
1010→                    indentConfig
1011→                        .toNormalizedIndent(nodeIndentWithoutAcceptableTrailingSpaces)
1012→                        .plus(acceptableTrailingSpaces)
1013→                } else {
1014→                    nodeIndent
1015→                }
1016→            }
1017→        }
1018→    }
1019→
1020→    private fun visitWhiteSpaceBeforeClosingQuote(
1021→        node: ASTNode,
1022→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
1023→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
1024→    ) {
1025→        if (!this::stringTemplateIndenter.isInitialized) {
1026→            stringTemplateIndenter = StringTemplateIndenter(indentConfig)
1027→        }
1028→        stringTemplateIndenter.visitClosingQuotes(currentIndent(), node.treeParent, autoCorrect, emit)
1029→    }
1030→
1031→    private fun ASTNode.acceptableTrailingSpaces(): String {
1032→        require(elementType == WHITE_SPACE)
1033→        val acceptableTrailingSpaces = when (nextLeaf()?.elementType) {
1034→            KDOC_LEADING_ASTERISK, KDOC_END -> {
1035→                // The indentation of a KDoc comment contains a space as the last character regardless of the indentation
1036→                // style (tabs or spaces) except for the starting line of the KDoc comment
1037→                KDOC_CONTINUATION_INDENT
1038→            }
1039→
1040→            TYPE_CONSTRAINT -> {
1041→                // 6 spaces (length of "where" keyword plus a separator space) to indent type constraints as below:
1042→                //    where A1 : RecyclerView.Adapter<V1>,
1043→                //               A1 : ComposableAdapter.ViewTypeProvider,
1044→                TYPE_CONSTRAINT_CONTINUATION_INDENT
1045→            }
1046→
1047→            else -> ""
1048→        }
1049→        val nodeIndent = text.substringAfterLast("\n")
1050→        return if (nodeIndent.endsWith(acceptableTrailingSpaces)) {
1051→            return acceptableTrailingSpaces
1052→        } else {
1053→            ""
1054→        }
1055→    }

1057→    private fun startNoIndentZone(node: ASTNode) =
1058→        IndentContext(
1059→            fromASTNode = node,
1060→            nodeIndent = "",
1061→            firstChildIndent = "",
1062→            childIndent = "",
1063→            lastChildIndent = "",
1064→            activated = true,
1065→        )
1066→
1067→    private companion object {
1068→        const val KDOC_CONTINUATION_INDENT = " "
1069→        const val TYPE_CONSTRAINT_CONTINUATION_INDENT = "      " // Length of keyword "where" plus separating space
1070→    }
1071→
1072→    private data class IndentContext(
1073→        /**
1074→         * The node on which the indent context starts.
1075→         */
1076→        val fromASTNode: ASTNode,
1077→
1078→        /**
1079→         * The node at which the indent context ends. If null, then the context ends at the last child leaf of the node
1080→         * on which the indent context starts.
1081→         */
1082→        val toASTNode: ASTNode = fromASTNode.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
1083→
1084→        /**
1085→         * Cumulative indentation for the node. Normally this should be equal to a multiple of the
1086→         * 'indentConfig.indentStyle' to ensure a consistent indentation style.
1087→         */
1088→        val nodeIndent: String,
1089→
1090→        /**
1091→         * Additional indentation for first child node. Normally this should be equal to the 'indentConfig.indent' to
1092→         * ensure a consistent indentation style. In very limited cases when the default indentation is set to 'tab' it
1093→         * is still needed to adjust the last indentation using spaces.
1094→         */
1095→        val firstChildIndent: String,
1096→
1097→        /**
1098→         * Additional indentation for child nodes. Normally this should be equal to the 'indentConfig.indent' to ensure
1099→         * a consistent indentation style. In very limited cases when the default indentation is set to 'tab' it is
1100→         * still needed to adjust the last indentation using spaces.
1101→         */
1102→        val childIndent: String,
1103→
1104→        /**
1105→         * Additional indentation for last child node. Normally this should be equal to the 'indentConfig.indent' to
1106→         * ensure a consistent indentation style. In very limited cases when the default indentation is set to 'tab' it
1107→         * is still needed to adjust the last indentation using spaces.
1108→         */
1109→        val lastChildIndent: String,
1110→
1111→        /**
1112→         * True when the indentation level of this context is activated
1113→         */
1114→        val activated: Boolean = false,
1115→    ) {
1116→        val nodes: String
1117→            get() =
1118→                fromASTNode
1119→                    .prevLeaf() // The 'fromAstNode' itself needs to be returned by '.leaves()' call as well
1120→                    ?.leaves()
1121→                    .orEmpty()
1122→                    .takeWhile {
1123→                        // The 'toAstNode' itself needs to be included as well
1124→                        it != toASTNode.nextLeaf()
1125→                    }
1126→                    .joinToString(separator = "") { it.text }
1127→                    .textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()
1128→
1129→        fun indent() =
1130→            if (activated) {
1131→                nodeIndent + childIndent
1132→            } else {
1133→                nodeIndent
1134→            }
1135→
1136→        fun prevCodeLeaf() = fromASTNode.prevCodeLeaf()!!
1137→    }
1138→}

1140→private fun ASTNode.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline() = text.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()

1142→private fun String.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline(): String {
1143→    val (prefix, suffix) = if (this.all { it.isWhitespace() }) {
1144→        Pair("[", "]")
1145→    } else {
1146→        Pair("", "")
1147→    }
1148→    return prefix
1149→        .plus(
1150→            this
1151→                .replace("\t", "\\t")
1152→                .replace("\n", "\\n"),
1153→        ).plus(suffix)
1154→}

1156→private class StringTemplateIndenter(private val indentConfig: IndentConfig) {
1157→    fun visitClosingQuotes(
1158→        expectedIndent: String,
1159→        node: ASTNode,
1160→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
1161→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
1162→    ) {
1163→        require(node.elementType == STRING_TEMPLATE)
1164→        node
1165→            .let { it.psi as KtStringTemplateExpression }
1166→            .takeIf { it.isFollowedByTrimIndent() || it.isFollowedByTrimMargin() }
1167→            ?.takeIf { it.isMultiLine() }
1168→            ?.let { _ ->
1169→                if (node.containsMixedIndentationCharacters()) {
1170→                    // It can not be determined with certainty how mixed indentation characters should be interpreted.
1171→                    // The trimIndent function handles tabs and spaces equally (one tabs equals one space) while the user
1172→                    // might expect that the tab size in the indentation is more than one space.
1173→                    emit(
1174→                        node.startOffset,
1175→                        "Indentation of multiline string should not contain both tab(s) and space(s)",
1176→                        false,
1177→                    )
1178→                    return
1179→                }
1180→
1181→                val prefixLength = node.getCommonPrefixLength()
1182→                val correctedExpectedIndent =
1183→                    if (node.prevLeaf()?.text == "\n") {
1184→                        // In case the opening quotes are placed at the start of the line, then the closing quotes
1185→                        // should have no indent as well.
1186→                        ""
1187→                    } else {
1188→                        expectedIndent
1189→                    }
1190→                node.children()
1191→                    .forEach {
1192→                        if (it.prevLeaf()?.text == "\n" &&
1193→                            (
1194→                                it.isLiteralStringTemplateEntry() ||
1195→                                    it.isVariableStringTemplateEntry() ||
1196→                                    it.isClosingQuote()
1197→                                )
1198→                        ) {
1199→                            val (actualIndent, actualContent) =
1200→                                if (it.isIndentBeforeClosingQuote()) {
1201→                                    it.text.splitIndentAt(it.text.length)
1202→                                } else if (it.isVariableStringTemplateEntry() && it.isFirstNonBlankElementOnLine()) {
1203→                                    it.getFirstElementOnSameLine().text.splitIndentAt(correctedExpectedIndent.length)
1204→                                } else {
1205→                                    it.text.splitIndentAt(prefixLength)
1206→                                }
1207→                            if (indentConfig.containsUnexpectedIndentChar(actualIndent)) {
1208→                                val offsetFirstWrongIndentChar =
1209→                                    indentConfig.indexOfFirstUnexpectedIndentChar(actualIndent)
1210→                                emit(
1211→                                    it.startOffset + offsetFirstWrongIndentChar,
1212→                                    "Unexpected '${indentConfig.unexpectedIndentCharDescription}' character(s) in margin of multiline " +
1213→                                        "string",
1214→                                    true,
1215→                                )
1216→                                if (autoCorrect) {
1217→                                    (it.firstChildNode as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(
1218→                                        correctedExpectedIndent + actualContent,
1219→                                    )
1220→                                }
1221→                            } else if (actualIndent != correctedExpectedIndent && it.isIndentBeforeClosingQuote()) {
1222→                                // It is a deliberate choice not to fix the indents inside the string literal except the line which only contains
1223→                                // the closing quotes.
1224→                                emit(
1225→                                    it.startOffset,
1226→                                    "Unexpected indent of multiline string closing quotes",
1227→                                    true,
1228→                                )
1229→                                if (autoCorrect) {
1230→                                    if (it.firstChildNode == null) {
1231→                                        (it as LeafPsiElement).rawInsertBeforeMe(
1232→                                            LeafPsiElement(REGULAR_STRING_PART, correctedExpectedIndent),
1233→                                        )
1234→                                    } else {
1235→                                        (it.firstChildNode as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(
1236→                                            correctedExpectedIndent + actualContent,
1237→                                        )
1238→                                    }
1239→                                }
1240→                            }
1241→                        }
1242→                    }
1243→            }
1244→    }
1245→
1246→    /**
1247→     * Get the length of the indent which is shared by all lines inside the string template except for the indent of
1248→     * the closing quotes.
1249→     */
1250→    private fun ASTNode.getCommonPrefixLength() =
1251→        children()
1252→            .filterNot { it.elementType == OPEN_QUOTE }
1253→            .filterNot { it.elementType == CLOSING_QUOTE }
1254→            .filter { it.prevLeaf()?.text == "\n" }
1255→            .filterNot { it.text == "\n" }
1256→            .let { indents ->
1257→                val indentsExceptBlankIndentBeforeClosingQuote = indents
1258→                    .filterNot { it.isIndentBeforeClosingQuote() }
1259→                if (indentsExceptBlankIndentBeforeClosingQuote.count() > 0) {
1260→                    indentsExceptBlankIndentBeforeClosingQuote
1261→                } else {
1262→                    indents
1263→                }
1264→            }
1265→            .map { it.text.indentLength() }
1266→            .minOrNull()
1267→            ?: 0
1268→
1269→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedByTrimIndent() = isFollowedBy("trimIndent()")
1270→
1271→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedByTrimMargin() = isFollowedBy("trimMargin()")
1272→
1273→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedBy(callExpressionName: String) =
1274→        this.node.nextSibling { it.elementType != DOT }
1275→            .let { it?.elementType == CALL_EXPRESSION && it.text == callExpressionName }
1276→
1277→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isMultiLine(): Boolean {
1278→        for (child in node.children()) {
1279→            if (child.elementType == LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY) {
1280→                val v = child.text
1281→                if (v == "\n") {
1282→                    return true
1283→                }
1284→            }
1285→        }
1286→        return false
1287→    }
1288→
1289→    private fun ASTNode.containsMixedIndentationCharacters(): Boolean {
1290→        assert((this.psi as KtStringTemplateExpression).isMultiLine())
1291→        val nonBlankLines = this
1292→            .text
1293→            .split("\n")
1294→            .filterNot { it.startsWith("\"\"\"") }
1295→            .filterNot { it.endsWith("\"\"\"") }
1296→            .filterNot { it.isBlank() }
1297→        val prefixLength = nonBlankLines.minOfOrNull { it.indentLength() } ?: 0
1298→        val distinctIndentCharacters = nonBlankLines
1299→            .joinToString(separator = "") {
1300→                it.splitIndentAt(prefixLength).first
1301→            }
1302→            .toCharArray()
1303→            .distinct()
1304→            .count()
1305→        return distinctIndentCharacters > 1
1306→    }
1307→
1308→    private fun ASTNode.isIndentBeforeClosingQuote() =
1309→        elementType == CLOSING_QUOTE || (text.isBlank() && nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == CLOSING_QUOTE)
1310→
1311→    private fun ASTNode.isLiteralStringTemplateEntry() = elementType == LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY && text != "\n"
1312→
1313→    private fun ASTNode.isVariableStringTemplateEntry() =
1314→        elementType == LONG_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY || elementType == SHORT_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY
1315→
1316→    private fun ASTNode.isClosingQuote() = elementType == CLOSING_QUOTE
1317→
1318→    private fun String.indentLength() = indexOfFirst { !it.isWhitespace() }.let { if (it == -1) length else it }
1319→
1320→    /**
1321→     * Splits the string at the given index or at the first non-white space character before that index. The returned pair
1322→     * consists of the indentation and the second part contains the remainder. Note that the second part still can start
1323→     * with whitespace characters in case the original strings starts with more white space characters than the requested
1324→     * split index.
1325→     */
1326→    private fun String.splitIndentAt(index: Int): Pair<String, String> {
1327→        assert(index >= 0)
1328→        val firstNonWhitespaceIndex = indexOfFirst { !it.isWhitespace() }.let {
1329→            if (it == -1) {
1330→                this.length
1331→            } else {
1332→                it
1333→            }
1334→        }
1335→        val safeIndex = kotlin.math.min(firstNonWhitespaceIndex, index)
1336→        return Pair(
1337→            first = this.take(safeIndex),
1338→            second = this.substring(safeIndex),
1339→        )
1340→    }
1341→
1342→    private fun ASTNode.getFirstElementOnSameLine(): ASTNode {
1343→        val firstLeafOnLine = prevLeaf { it.text == "\n" }
1344→        return if (firstLeafOnLine == null) {
1345→            this
1346→        } else {
1347→            firstLeafOnLine.nextLeaf(includeEmpty = true) ?: this
1348→        }
1349→    }
1350→
1351→    private fun ASTNode.isFirstNonBlankElementOnLine(): Boolean {
1352→        var node: ASTNode? = getFirstElementOnSameLine()
1353→        while (node != null && node != this && node.text.isWhitespace()) {
1354→            node = node.nextLeaf()
1355→        }
1356→        return node != this
1357→    }
1358→
1359→    private fun String.isWhitespace() = none { !it.isWhitespace() }
1360→}

1362→public val INDENTATION_RULE_ID: RuleId = IndentationRule().ruleId

 86→                .hasLintViolationWithoutAutoCorrect(2, 1, "Wildcard import")
 87→        }
 88→
 89→        @Test
 90→        fun `Given that property is set without a value then the packages which otherwise would be accepted by IntelliJ Default are detected`() {
 91→            val code =
 92→                """
 93→                import a.b
 94→                import kotlinx.android.synthetic.main.layout_name.*
 95→                """.trimIndent()
 96→            noWildcardImportsRuleAssertThat(code)
 97→                .withEditorConfigOverride(IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND to "")
 98→                .hasLintViolationWithoutAutoCorrect(2, 1, "Wildcard import")
